other_congregational_responsibilities.other_congregational_responsibilities_he:
At Grace Lutheran Church (Grand Rapids, MI):
- Adult Education Coordinator (2017–present): Responsible for planning and coordinating the adult education calendar, including midweek Bible classes, Sunday morning Bible studies, and periodic topical seminars (e.g., financial stewardship, parenting, and Christian vocation). I work closely with the pastor to select curriculum, recruit teachers, and ensure that materials are ordered and available. A specific example is a 12-week series on the Gospel of John that I developed with detailed handouts, weekly discussion questions, and a reading plan.

- Visitation Ministry Coordinator (2018–present): Organize and participate in visits to homebound members, those in nursing homes, and members recovering from surgery or illness. I maintain a detailed spreadsheet of members’ needs, preferred visiting times, and any mobility or communication concerns. I schedule monthly visits for a team of six volunteers and personally visit about 6–8 individuals per month, often bringing printed sermons, Portals of Prayer, or recorded services on USB drives for those who cannot attend worship.

- Confirmation Mentor (2016–2022): Paired with 7th–8th grade confirmands to meet twice a month during the school year for catechism review, prayer, and conversation about applying their faith in daily life (school, friendships, social media, etc.). I kept a journal of our meetings to track topics covered and prayer requests, and I regularly communicated with parents to ensure they knew what we were discussing.

- Worship Assistant / Lector (2010–present): Regularly serve as liturgical assistant for Sunday services, reading Scripture lessons, assisting with the distribution of Holy Communion under the pastor’s direction, and occasionally leading portions of the liturgy (e.g., prayers of the church) when requested.

At St. Mark Lutheran Church (Kentwood, MI):
- Youth Ministry Volunteer (2003–2009): Helped organize weekly youth group meetings, planned annual retreats to Camp Arcadia, and coordinated service projects such as food drives for the local pantry and yard cleanup days for elderly members. I often drove the church van for events and handled permission forms and parent communication.

- Congregational Treasurer’s Assistant (2006–2008): Assisted the volunteer treasurer with data entry, monthly reconciliation of checking accounts, and preparation of summary reports for the church council. Helped transition the congregation from a paper ledger system to a basic accounting software package, ensuring historical records were accurately migrated.

At Trinity Lutheran Church (Grand Rapids, MI):
- College Student Representative (1998–2000): Served as a liaison between the congregation and local college students, helping organize rides to church, Sunday lunches for students, and occasional study nights at the church fellowship hall during exam weeks.

Across congregations:
- Retreat and Workshop Leader: Have led several half-day and full-day retreats for men’s groups and mixed adult groups, focusing on topics like Christian leadership in the workplace, balancing family and church commitments, and spiritual disciplines (prayer, Scripture reading, Sabbath rest). I typically prepare detailed outlines, printed handouts, and follow-up resource lists for participants.

- Technology and A/V Support: Assisted with upgrading sound systems, setting up livestreaming during and after the COVID-19 pandemic, training volunteers on running projection slides, and documenting step-by-step instructions so that new volunteers can be brought up to speed quickly.
